item.title: Two children wounded in bomb explosion
item.description: Two children were wounded as a bomb exploded while they were playing with it at Kholadanga Gazipara in Jashore town at about 3:00pm on May 28, said Masud Khan, officer in-charge, Kotwali police station....

item.title: Youth found hanging in Chuadanga
item.description: THE police recovered the hanging body of a young man from a bamboo grove in Darshana upazila of Chuadanga on Sunday morning....

item.title: 3-month Sundarban visitor ban starts today
item.description: The government has imposed a three-month ban on all entry into Sundarban from June 1 to August 31 to protect the breeding season of wildlife, fisheries resources and biodiversity in the world’s largest mangrove forest....

item.title: IUB ranks 1st among private univs in NIR
item.description: Independent University, Bangladesh has emerged as Bangladesh’s top-ranked private university in the Nature Index institutional rankings, placing fifth overall among Bangladeshi institutions and third among universities countrywide....

item.title: Sylhet-Guwahati direct flight to be launched soon
item.description: Commerce minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir said on Sunday that a direct flight of Biman Bangladesh Airlines would be launched soon between Sylhet and India’s Guwahati....

item.title: Complaint filed against couple over house help torture
item.description: A domestic worker in Sirajganj has alleged that she was physically and mentally abused after being accused of theft by her employers, a doctor and his lecturer wife....

item.title: 10 held while entering Bangladesh
item.description: Members of the Border Guard Bangladesh detained 10 people, including three women and five children, allegedly for entering Bangladesh illegally through the Darshana border in Chuadanga early Sunday....

item.title: Eid joy eludes northern char dwellers
item.description: This year Eid-ul-Azha brought little more than anxiety, hardship and uncertainty, instead of festivity, for thousands of people living in the riverine chars of northern Bangladesh....

item.title: Several city cattle markets not cleaned
item.description: Temporary cattle market wastages were found not cleaned along various city streets while sacrificial animal wastages were seen largely removed on Sunday, the third day after the first day of Eid-ul-Azha, one of the biggest religious festivals of the Muslims....

item.title: Freedom fighter Imamul Kabir Shanto’s death anniv observed
item.description: The sixth anniversary of death of freedom fighter Md Imamul Kabir Shanto, founder chairman of Shanto-Mariam Foundation, Shanto-Mariam University of Creative Technology and Sundarban Courier Service Pvt Limited, was observed on Saturday....

item.title: Art Camp ‘Jal Paharer Jal Kabyo’ held at Susang Durgapur
item.description: A two-day art camp titled Jal Paharer Jal Kabyo, featuring 11 seasoned and promising artists, concluded on Sunday at Susang Durgapur in Netrakona....

item.title: Social welfare minister shares Eid joy with underprivileged children
item.description: Social welfare and women and children affairs minister AZM Zahid Hossain exchanged post-Eid greetings with the underprivileged children at his residence on Bayley Road in Dhaka on Sunday....

item.title: PSG, Enrique do it again
item.description: If a rock-solid Arsenal could not stop Luis Enrique and Paris Saint-Germain, one steward clutching a rope stood no chance......

item.title: Meteor explodes over US with 300 tons force
item.description: A meteor crashing toward Earth exploded over the northeastern United States on Saturday, NASA said, setting off booms that echoed over the region with a blast equivalent to 300 tons of TNT....

item.title: Bangladesh leave for San Marino
item.description: Bangladesh national football team left Dhaka on Sunday for their first-ever international friendly on European soil against San Marino, which is scheduled to be held at the San Marino Stadium on June 5......

item.title: Jamaat to offer shadow cabinet, budget proposal
item.description: The Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami has finalised a shadow cabinet, led by party amir Shafiqur Rahman, and is expected to announce it in June....

item.title: Neymar fit for ‘first or second’ match, says Ancelotti
item.description: Brazil star Neymar is on course to shake off a calf strain and could even be ready for their opening match of the World Cup, coach Carlo Ancelotti said on Saturday......

item.title: Bangladesh suffer defeat to China
item.description: Bangladesh suffered a narrow 3-2 defeat to China in their Pool B second match of the men’s U-18 Asia Cup Hockey at the Kawasaki Heavy Industries Hockey Stadium in Kakamigahara, Japan on Sunday......

item.title: Drone hits captured nuclear plant in Ukraine: IAEA
item.description: A drone hit the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in Russian-occupied southern Ukraine, the UN nuclear agency confirmed Sunday....

item.title: Liverpool sack Slot, Iraola in line to take over
item.description: Liverpool sacked manager Arne Slot on Saturday, saying the club were seeking a ‘change of direction’, after a disastrous defence of their Premier League title......

item.title: Yamal admits he feared missing WC
item.description: Spanish national team star Lamine Yamal admitted Sunday he had been ‘afraid’ of missing the World Cup because of the hamstring injury he is still recovering from......

item.title: Dhaka to bring up border killing, push-in at BGB-BSF Delhi summit
item.description: The four-day director general-level talks between the Border Guard Bangladesh and the Indian Border Security Force will be held in New Delhi from June 8 to June 11....

item.title: PALLABI CHILD RAPE, MURDER: Charge hearing today
item.description: The parents and sister of the eight-year-old girl who was allegedly raped and murdered in the capital’s Pallabi area met with the prosecutor assigned to the case on Sunday, ahead of today’s charge hearing in the case....

item.title: Infant deaths expose weak health governance
item.description: THE deaths of newborns in a private hospital in the capital yet again shows the fragility of the health system. Six newborn aged between one and three days died on May 27 in a few hours after their conditions deteriorated in the post-operative room at Ad-din Hospital. The hospital authorities and police speculate that the deaths may have been caused by a technical failure......

item.title: It’s time govt restored police effectiveness
item.description: A SURGE in attacks on law enforcers, mostly by crime suspects during raids and drives, is worrying. Police data show 213 incidents of attack to have happened on law enforcers in January–April, with 42 each in January and February, 63 in March and 66 in April. The attacks have not only caused injuries to law enforcers. Some of them have died in such attacks. Police data show......

item.title: Geopolitical stakes
item.description: ON JUNE 2, delegates from the 193 member states of the United Nations are scheduled to meet to hold an election to choose a new president for the 81st session of the United Nations General Assembly. Unlike other positions in the UN, the role of the General Assembly president, though still important, is mostly ceremonial. It pales in significance compared to, for......

item.title: Ganges treaty needs substance
item.description: THE current Ganges River Water Sharing Agreement between India and Bangladesh is set to expire at the end of 2026, when there will again be extensive discussions about friendship, regional cooperation and historical ties between the two countries. Diplomats will speak of ‘shared rivers’ and ‘civilisational bonds,’ while political leaders will rely on the language of......

item.title: Can the rich survive without the poor?
item.description: AS PEOPLE across the country were preparing to celebrate Eid, news of a horrific tragedy swept across the media. Early in the morning on May 25, fifteen home-bound people were killed when the overloaded open truck they were using as a cheap ride flipped over, crushing them to death under tons of steel rods with which the truck was loaded. And they were all street......

item.title: Interval of time
item.description: IN 1921, a few years into the Soviet experiment, VI Lenin published an essay with the revealing title ‘New Times and Old Mistakes in a New Guise.’ The essay opened a line of inquiry that would remain with Lenin until the end of his life three years later. What captivated him was the issue of how to build socialism in a country ravaged by war, with minimal capital at its......

item.title: Man killed in Bogura road accident
item.description: The headmaster of a government primary school was killed in a road accident in Shibganj upazila of Bogura district on Saturday night......

item.title: Three family members found dead in Khulna, case filed
item.description: A case has been filed in connection with the killing of a woman and her two grandsons in Khulna city, while police have intensified efforts to arrest the prime suspect, believed to be the stepfather of the two children....

item.title: Elderly woman, her two grandchildren found dead at Khulna house
item.description: Police have recovered the bodies of three members of a family from a rented house in Sonadanga area of Khulna city......

item.title: Man stabs sister-in-law, nephew to death over family dispute in Manikganj
item.description: A man allegedly hacked his sister-in-law and her infant son to death following a family dispute in Daulatpur upazila of Manikganj district in an attack, which also left the victim's husband injured......

item.title: Former England star Sterling arrested
item.description: Raheem Sterling has been arrested on suspicion of drug-driving after he allegedly crashed his car into motorway barriers, police said Saturday......

item.title: Dhaka sees moderate air with AQI score of 96 Sunday morning
item.description: Dhaka ranked 13th among the world’s most polluted cities on Sunday morning, recording an Air Quality Index of 96 at about 10:50am......

item.title: Japan takes swipe at China at security meet
item.description: Japan’s defence minister took a veiled swipe at China on Sunday, pledging to keep strengthening the military despite Beijing’s criticism of Tokyo’s increasingly muscular security stance......

item.title: WHO chief visits epicentre of Ebola outbreak in DRC
item.description: UN health chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus called Saturday for more international help to combat the spread of the deadly Ebola virus in Democratic Republic of Congo as he arrived in the eastern province worst-hit by a severe outbreak......

item.title: Israel plants flag on medieval castle
item.description: Israel’s flag flew over the medieval castle of Beaufort in Lebanon on Sunday, as it warned Lebanese civilians to evacuate a large part of the south of the country ahead of stepped up ground operations....

item.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian trying to climb barrier
item.description: Israeli forces shot dead a Palestinian man Sunday as he attempted to enter Jerusalem by climbing over a barrier separating the city from the occupied West Bank, Palestinian authorities said......

item.title: Malta’s Labour party wins 4th term
item.description: Malta’s Labour party won an unprecedented fourth term Sunday in a victory for outgoing prime minister Robert Abela, who had called a snap election in light of geopolitical uncertainties......

item.title: A world away for families split by Kashmir unrest
item.description: Kashmir resident Raja Basharat can see his brother’s grave on the opposite bank of a river that divides the disputed region, but visiting it — a holiday tradition for Muslims on Eid al-Adha — is impossible......

item.title: TMC MP claims BJP supporters attacked him
item.description: TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee claimed he suffered head injuries after being mobbed in front of a police station he visited in West Bengal’s Hooghly district on Sunday to submit a memorandum against the arrest of party workers after the Assembly elections, reports The Hindu......

item.title: Cumilla dist administrator’s distorted remarks on Asif, Hasnat spark controversy
item.description: Distorted remarks by Cumilla Zila Parishad administrator and BNP leader Mostak Mia alleging that National Citizen Party spokesperson Asif Mahmud Shojib Bhuyain and the party’s chief organiser (southern region), Hasnat Abdullah, received Tk 25 crore from......

item.title: Cumilla dist council chief’s distorted remarks on Asif, Hasnat spark controversy
item.description: Remarks by Cumilla Zila Parishad administrator and BNP leader Mostak Mia alleging that National Citizen Party spokesperson Asif Mahmud Shojib Bhuyain and the party’s chief organiser (southern region), Hasnat Abdullah, received Tk 25 crore from the council’s funds have triggered widespread controversy....

item.title: Asif Mahmud took Tk 15cr, Hasnat Tk 10cr from Cumilla Zila Parishad, claims administrator
item.description: Cumilla Zila Parishad administrator Mostak Mia has claimed that National Citizen Party spokesperson Asif Mahmud Shojib Bhuyain and chief organiser (Southern region) Hasnat Abdullah took a total of Tk 25 crore from the parishad’s revenue fund......

item.title: Road accidents kill 136 during Eid holiday
item.description: At least 136 people were killed and 302 others were injured in road accidents across the country during the public holidays marking the recently concluded Eid-ul-Azha....

item.title: Iran shutters cafe over alleged ‘satanic’ activities
item.description: Iranian authorities have shut down a cafe in central Tehran over allegations that it promoted ‘satanic’ activities, local media reported on Sunday......

item.title: Dublin protest urges probe into ‘George Floyd’-style death
item.description: Protesters in Dublin demanded ‘justice’ Saturday for a Congolese man who died after he was restrained by security guards, in an incident likened to George Floyd’s murder in the United States six years ago......

item.title: DEATH OF NEWBORNS AT AD-DIN: DGHS finds irregularities, negligence
item.description: Government authorities and probe committees formed following the deaths of six newborns at Ad-Din Medical College Hospital in the capital’s Moghbazar area on May 27 have found indications of irregularities and negligence by the hospital authorities in providing services....

item.title: Mob violence kills 32, injures 71 in May: report
item.description: Human rights organisation Manabadhikar Shongskriti Foundation in its monthly monitoring report has marked a significant rise in the incidents of rape and mob violence in May compared with the previous month of April....

item.title: NHS cancer jab eradicates tumours
item.description: A cancer injection used by the UK’s National Health Service has eradicated tumours in an ‘unprecedented’ clinical trial, according to The Telegraph......

item.title: Formal wages for 67 more sectors likely
item.description: The Minimum Wages Board has identified 67 additional industrial sectors and proposed to bring them under formal wage regulation as part of efforts to expand labour protection and formalise employment across the country....

item.title: Bank deposits grow 11.76pc on higher rates, remittances
item.description: Bank deposit growth surged by 11.76 per cent year-on-year in March, the fastest pace in years, as rising remittances and higher interest rates pulled fresh money into the banking system......

item.title: Rangamati tourism rebounds as Eid holidaymakers flock to hill district
item.description: The scenic hill district of Rangamati is witnessing a massive influx of tourists during the Eid holidays, bringing renewed life to the region’s tourism sector after months of sluggish activity......

item.title: VDP steps in to preserve rawhides
item.description: Bangladesh Ansar and Village Defence Party has launched a special initiative to preserve sacrificial animal hides during Eid-ul-Azha to protect national resources and strengthen the country’s leather industry as around 50 to 55 percent of the country’s annual rawhide is collected during this session......

item.description: The Chattogram Port Authority has constituted three distinct taskforces to facilitating smooth unloading of goods at both the jetty and outer anchorage throughout the extended Eid holidays......

item.title: Govt hikes petrol, octane prices by Tk 5, diesel price unchanged
item.description: The government on Sunday increased the prices of octane and petrol by Tk 5 per litre....

item.title: Japan spends $73b in yen interventions
item.description: The Bank of Japan spent around 11.7 trillion yen, equivalent to $73 billion, in the past month to prop up the yen, official government data showed on Friday......

item.title: Rawhide price slump brings miseries to small traders
item.description: The market for sacrificial animal hides has witnessed another collapse this year, as seasonal traders and small collectors had to sell hides at well below the government-fixed prices....

item.title: Sri Lanka raises fuel prices after IMF loan instalment
item.description: Sri Lanka raised fuel prices by up to 6 per cent on Sunday, in line with IMF plans, to recover energy costs and phase out subsidies to stabilise the economy......

item.description: Women empowerment is a much-discussed issue in Bangladesh, and accordingly many endeavours, taken both in private and public sectors, are showing signs of making women financially solvent......

item.description: Artificial intelligence is getting expensive — and companies are starting to rethink their embrace of the disruptive technology......

item.title: EU wants to break up with US tech
item.description: Wary of being vulnerable to the whims of foreign governments, the European Union is preparing far-reaching new moves to ditch American digital companies and Chinese chips in favour of European alternatives......

item.title: Myanmar farmers face fertiliser, fuel dearth
item.description: Under monsoon skies leaden with rain, Myanmar rice farmers despondently tend their paddy fields, doubtful the deluge will bring bountiful harvests during a fuel and fertiliser crisis caused by the Iran war......

item.title: Europe’s green jet fuels see upside in Middle East war
item.description: At a plant near Frankfurt, in Germany, hydrogen and CO2 sourced from a chlorine factory and a biogas facility nearby are piped into a reactor and — after a few more steps — turned into jet fuel......

item.title: New gold rush threatens indigenous havens in Brazil’s Amazon
item.description: Indigenous chief Bepdjo Mekragnotire is once again preparing to lead a group of warriors to chase wildcat gold miners away from his people’s territory in Brazil’s Amazon rainforest......

item.title: UK risks a ‘lost generation’ of jobless young people
item.description: Britain risks creating a ‘lost generation’ as the number of young people out of work and education surges, a government-commissioned review warned Thursday......

item.title: Energy crunch fuels car pool growth
item.description: Rising fuel prices triggered by the Middle East war are driving a sharp increase in carpooling, with a ride-sharing platform reporting a surge in new users seeking cheaper ways to travel......

item.title: US, Mexico finish first round of trade deal talks
item.description: The United States and Mexico completed a first round of bilateral trade talks Friday, focused on revising the North American Free Trade Agreement in light of pressure from president Donald Trump’s tariff policies......

item.title: French GDP slips 0.1pc in Q1, raising spectre of recession
item.description: French GDP shrank 0.1 per cent in the first quarter, official data showed Friday, a downward revision from previous estimates that analysts warn could herald a recession for the eurozone’s second-biggest economy......

item.title: SoftBank to spend $87.5b on AI centres in France: founder
item.description: Japanese tech investor SoftBank will spend 75 billion euros ($87.5 billion) on artificial intelligence infrastructure in France, its founder Masayoshi Son told a French newspaper in an interview released Saturday......

item.title: Biggest EU economies agree on steps for financial integration
item.description: The finance ministers of the European Union’s six biggest economies said Friday they had agreed on steps to better integrate financial markets and make investments flow seamlessly across the bloc......

item.description: Nobel laureate Professor Muhammad Yunus has a long first-time meeting with newly elected Paris mayor Emmanuel Grégoire at his office in the historic Paris Building at the centre of the city on Friday......

item.description: Elon Musk insists that his artificial intelligence venture xAI remains a serious competitor, pushing back against mounting doubts after revelations that the supercomputing facilities built to power his own AI models are being rented out to a rival......

item.title: Mistral says would not interfere if its AI is used by defence customers
item.description: French AI startup Mistral would not weigh in on choices about how its technology is used by defence customers, its chief executive told AFP Thursday, laying out a clear position in an ethical debate stirring up the sector......

item.description: Bangladesh conceded a 3-0 defeat to India in their second and final Group B match of the SAFF Women’s Championship at the Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium in Goa, India on Sunday. ...

item.title: China’s May factory activity flat as weak demand slows growth
item.description: China’s factory activity was flat in May after two months of expansion, official data showed Sunday, as weaker demand and rising energy costs due to the Middle East war weighed on growth......

item.description: Mohammed Nurul Amin has been appointed as chairman of Bangladesh Krishi Bank’s board of directors on May 24, said a press release......

item.description: President Donald Trump has sought to change several terms of a proposal to end the Middle East war, United States media reported on Saturday, as a finalised deal remains elusive among the parties......

item.description: Nvidia chief executive Jensen Huang is expected to visit South Korea this week, a source familiar with the matter told AFP on Saturday, as the country rides a boom in artificial intelligence......

item.description: A blast in northern Myanmar killed dozens of people on Sunday, two rescue workers said, with rebels controlling the region saying it was caused by the accidental detonation of mining explosives....

item.description: Nigar Sultana Joty’s fighting knock was in vain as Bangladesh fell short by eight runs against the Netherlands in their second match of the tri-nation WT20I series at the Grange Cricket Club in Edinburgh on Sunday. ...

item.description: The surprising results, which were conducted ‘under very specific conditions’ in the lab, do not ‘call into question the effectiveness’ of the repellent DEET…...

item.description: A fire broke out at the old building of Pankhaiya Para High School in the Khagrachhari district town on Sunday afternoon....

item.description: Police on Saturday detained more than 280 people in Paris after violent clashes erupted when thousands poured onto the streets during Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League final victory......

item.title: Arsenal set aside loss to PSG with bus parade celebrating Premier League crown
item.description:
Thousands of Arsenal fans put their pain at missing out on European glory to one side Sunday as the club celebrated its first Premier League title since 2004 with a parade in London.

item.description: Prime minister and BNP chairman Tarique Rahman on Sunday warned party leaders and activists that a difficult and important time lies ahead, and called for their support in implementing the party’s election manifesto to make the government successful....

item.description: Bangladesh prime minister and BNP chairman Tarique Rahman on Sunday said that his government is committed to fulfilling all expectations of the country’s people....

item.description: Renowned Gono Sangeet singer, composer, lyricist, and music teacher Kamruddin Absar died on Saturday....

item.description: Renowned Gono Sangeet singer, composer, lyricist and music teacher Kamruddin Absar died on Saturday. He was 71....

item.description: Two people were killed and 15 others injured when a coach hit a stationary bus on the Sylhet–Dhaka Highway at Tajpur Kadamtala in the Osmaninagar upazila of Sylhet district on Sunday....

item.description: The last time Arsenal reached a Champions League final they lost it, and then it took 20 long years to claw their way back to that stage again....

item.description: The US Embassy in Dhaka has announced a faster visa processing system for immigrant visa applicants, introducing a two-day processing period from June 1....

item.description: Australian researchers have trained lab-grown brain cells on a silicon computer chip to play the nineties shooter game ‘Doom’ and say they are just scratching the surface of what the neurons could be capable of doing....

item.description: River ports in some regions have been asked to hoist cautionary signal 1 as a temporary west or northwesterly gusty or squally wind is likely to occur in many parts of the country, according to the special bulletin Bangladesh Meteorological Department......

item.title: Two more children die from measles in 24 hours taking death toll to 585
item.description: Two more children have died from measles-like symptoms in Bangladesh in 24 hours till 8:00am on Sunday, taking the total death toll to 585 since March 15, 2026......
